skill.name: rust-pro
skill.description: Guide Rust development with modern async patterns, type-safe APIs, production services, performance tuning, and safety-focused systems programming.
skill.summary: Expert guidance for building reliable, fast, and type-safe Rust applications.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported six weak cryptography alerts and one network reconnaissance alert in SKILL.md. Review found these are false positives in descriptive Rust guidance, with no executable code, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography alerts occur in ordinary prose about Rust skills, not in code that selects DES, MD5, RC4, or another weak algorithm. The referenced lines describe the skill, ownership and lifetimes, pattern destructuring, API design, web service design, and plugin system design.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain natural language only and no cryptographic implementation or algorithm selection. Confidence as a security risk is very low because no dangerous crypto behavior is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive Network Reconnaissance Match
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance alert points to a behavioral trait about using Rust's type system for compile-time correctness. No evidence found of port scanning, host discovery, network probing, or commands that perform reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is descriptive text and does not include network tooling, URLs, shell commands, or scanning logic. Confidence as a security risk is very low.

content.value_statement: Rust projects often fail when ownership, async design, or performance choices are unclear. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward safe APIs, tested services, and practical optimization.
actual_capabilities: Designs type-safe Rust APIs with explicit error handling.
Recommends async patterns using Tokio, axum, tower, and hyper.
Explains ownership, borrowing, lifetime, and trait design issues.
Plans testing with unit, integration, property, and benchmark coverage.
Guides performance work with profiling, memory layout, and cache-aware choices.
Reviews unsafe code and FFI designs for documented safety invariants.
limitations: Does not execute Rust code or verify compiler output by itself.
Requires project context for crate selection and architecture decisions.
Cannot guarantee compatibility with future Rust releases or crate versions.
Unsafe code guidance still requires expert human review before production use.

use_cases.title: Design an Async Service
use_cases.description: Plan a Rust web service with clear runtime choices, error handling, middleware, and testing strategy.

use_cases.title: Resolve Ownership Problems
use_cases.description: Analyze lifetime, borrowing, trait bound, and generic design issues in complex Rust code.

use_cases.title: Optimize Systems Code
use_cases.description: Improve performance with profiling guidance, memory layout review, and safe concurrency patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a Rust Error
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner support for ownership or compiler diagnostics.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain this Rust compiler error in simple terms. Show the likely ownership or lifetime issue and suggest a small, idiomatic fix.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Type-Safe API
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate design for library or service boundaries.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a type-safe Rust API for this feature. Include error types, trait boundaries, ownership model, and tests I should add.

prompt_templates.title: Review Async Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review for Tokio or web service design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this async Rust architecture. Check runtime choices, backpressure, cancellation, task ownership, and observability gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Performance Optimization
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced systems performance and safety review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Rust performance optimization plan for this code path. Include profiling steps, memory layout risks, concurrency options, and safety tradeoffs.

output_examples.input: Design an axum API for high-volume event ingestion.
output: A service architecture with router, extractor, middleware, and state boundaries.
A typed error model that separates client errors from internal failures.
A testing plan covering request validation, backpressure, and database failure paths.

output_examples.input: Fix a lifetime issue in a generic parser.
output: A plain-language explanation of why the borrowed value cannot outlive its owner.
Several possible fixes, including owned data, scoped borrows, or adjusted trait bounds.
Guidance on choosing the option with the smallest API impact.

output_examples.input: Improve throughput in a concurrent Rust pipeline.
output: A profiling-first workflow using benchmarks and runtime metrics.
Specific bottleneck hypotheses around allocation, locking, and channel pressure.
Safe optimization options with notes on complexity and verification.

best_practices: Provide the target Rust version, runtime constraints, and relevant crate versions.
Include compiler errors, benchmark results, or profiling data when asking for fixes.
Ask for tests and safety invariants when code uses concurrency, FFI, or unsafe blocks.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for unsafe optimizations without explaining the safety requirements.
Do not hide compiler errors or dependency versions when debugging Rust issues.
Do not choose async frameworks before defining latency, throughput, and deployment needs.

faq.question: Can this skill help beginners learn Rust?
faq.answer: Yes. It can explain ownership, borrowing, lifetimes, errors, and idiomatic patterns in practical terms.

faq.question: Does it run cargo commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and plans, but command execution depends on the host AI tool.

faq.question: Which Rust frameworks does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Tokio, axum, tower, hyper, warp, actix-web, sqlx, diesel, tonic, and related ecosystem tools.

faq.question: Can it review unsafe Rust?
faq.answer: Yes. It can identify required safety invariants and review designs, but production unsafe code needs expert review.

faq.question: Is it useful for performance work?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest profiling steps, benchmark structure, memory layout checks, and concurrency improvements.

faq.question: Can it design complete Rust services?
faq.answer: Yes. It can plan service architecture, error handling, tests, observability, and deployment-aware tradeoffs.
